bullets.text: Written premium grew 7% in Q1 2010, the best growth rate in 19 quarters; all business components contributed well and met targets, except Commercial Auto which was still down on volume.
Combined ratio for Q1 was just under 91, reflecting an underwriting margin of over nine points; profitability was broadly distributed across products and states with no significant cross-subsidies.
New application growth was strong across all business lines in Q1; Direct auto and Internet sales remained strong; Agency auto production from late 2009 continued into Q1 2010.
Direct auto remained a strong growth source especially via Internet; Agency auto production continued its positive trend from the second half of 2009 with strong conversion in the channel.
Personal injury protection states remained on the watch list though much of the expected improvement from prior corrective actions materialized during Q1; Florida was still outside targets.
Total return on investments was a respectable 3.2% for the quarter; unrealized gains of $964 million; debt-to-total capital ratio was 26%; Professional Liability Group sale completed during Q1.
Expense ratio of 21.7 remained somewhat elevated; however, policies-in-force per FTE improved 12% versus prior year, continuing significant gains from the restructuring of the previous two years.
